name: autoblocksai
documentation:
📚 Documentation   •   🖥️ Application   •   🏠 Home

## Installation ```bash poetry add autoblocksai ``` ```bash pip install autoblocksai ``` ## Examples See our [Python](https://github.com/autoblocksai/autoblocks-examples#python) examples. ## Documentation See [the full documentation](https://docs.autoblocks.ai). ## Issues / Questions Please [open an issue](https://github.com/autoblocksai/python-sdk/issues/new) if you encounter any bugs, have any questions, or have any feature requests. ## Contributing See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.context.PromptExecutionContext.render
documentation: @deprecated use `render_template` instead

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.cli.main.read_config
documentation: Reads YAML configuration from the specified path.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.cli.main.prompts
documentation: Handle prompts-related commands.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.placeholders.parse_placeholders_from_template
documentation:
Extracts placeholders from a template string. Placeholders look like: {{ param }}

Placeholders can be escaped with a leading backslash: \{{ escaped }}

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.placeholders.TemplatePlaceholder.name
documentation: the name of the placeholder (the text between the curly braces)

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.placeholders.TemplatePlaceholder.is_escaped
documentation: true if this placeholder is preceded by an escape character

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.manager.is_testing_context
documentation:
Note that we check for the presence of the CLI environment
variable and not the test case contextvars because the
contextvars aren't set until run_test_suite is called,
whereas a prompt manager might have already been imported
and initialized by the time run_test_suite is called.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.manager.prompt_revisions_map
documentation:
The AUTOBLOCKS_OVERRIDES_PROMPT_REVISIONS environment variable is a JSON-stringified
map of prompt IDs to revision IDs. This is set in CI test runs triggered
from the UI.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.manager.AutoblocksPromptManager._set_prompt_revision
documentation:
If this prompt has a revision override set, use the /validate endpoint to
check if the major version this prompt manager is configured to use is compatible
to be overridden with the revision.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.manager.AutoblocksPromptManager._refresh_loop
documentation:
Refreshes the latest version of the prompt every `refresh_interval` seconds.

This isn't exactly "every n seconds" but more "run and then wait n seconds",
which will cause it to drift each run by the time it takes to run the refresh,
but I think that is ok in this scenario.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.manager.AutoblocksPromptManager.exec
documentation:
See https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/PY-36444/PyCharm-doesnt-infer-types-when-using-contextlib.contextmanager-decorator
for why this wraps a context manager instead of being a context manager itself.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.models.PromptParams
documentation: LLM model parameters for a prompt.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.models.PromptTemplate
documentation: An individual template for a prompt.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.models.Prompt

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.models.Prompt.tracking
documentation: Returns the schema expected by our ingestion endpoint's prompt tracking property.
A schema representing a prompt managed in the Autoblocks platform.

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.models.RemoteConfig.major_version
documentation: should be a specific major version

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.models.RemoteConfig.minor_version
documentation: should be a specific minor version or "latest"

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.models.RemoteConfig.dangerously_use_undeployed_revision
documentation: can be a specific revision id or "latest"

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.autogenerate.make_prompts_from_config
documentation:
Fetches prompt data from the Autoblocks API and generates code for each prompt
configured in the .autoblocks.yml config file.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.prompts.autogenerate.write_generated_code_for_config
documentation: Write generated code to outfile.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.is_testing_context
documentation:
Note that we check for the presence of the CLI environment
variable and not the test case contextvars because the
contextvars aren't set until run_test_suite is called,
whereas a config manager might have already been imported
and initialized by the time run_test_suite is called.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.config_revisions_map
documentation:
The AUTOBLOCKS_OVERRIDES_CONFIG_REVISIONS environment variable is a JSON-stringified
map of config IDs to revision IDs. This is set in CI test runs triggered
from the UI.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.is_remote_config_refreshable
documentation:
A config is refreshable if the user has specified that they want the latest deployed config
or the latest undeployed config.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.AutoblocksConfig._refresh_loop
documentation:
Refreshes the latest version of the config every `refresh_interval` seconds.

This isn't exactly "every n seconds" but more "run and then wait n seconds",
which will cause it to drift each run by the time it takes to run the refresh,
but I think that is ok in this scenario.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.AutoblocksConfig._load_and_set_remote_config
documentation:
Loads the remote config from Autoblocks and sets the value of this config
based on the result.

If the parser is specified, the value will be parsed using the parser.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.AutoblocksConfig.activate_from_remote
documentation: Activate a remote config from Autoblocks and optionally refresh it every `refresh_interval` seconds.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.configs.config.AutoblocksConfig.stop_refreshing
documentation: Stops the config from automatically refreshing.

Run a list of evaluators on an event and return the evaluations as a list of dictionaries.

This is suffixed with _unsafe because it doesn't handle exceptions.
Its caller will catch and handle all exceptions.

documentation: Map your test case to a string for comparison.

documentation: Map your output to a string for comparison.

The IsEquals evaluator checks if the output equals the expected output.
The comparison is case-sensitive. Scores 1 if it is equal, 0 otherwise.

documentation: Map the test_case to an expected string to pass to the LLM judge.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.accuracy.BaseAccuracy.example_output_mapper
documentation:
Map an EvaluationOverride to a string representation of the output.
This gets passed to the LLM judge as an example.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.accuracy.BaseAccuracy.example_expected_output_mapper
documentation:
Map an EvaluationOverride to a string representation of the expected output.
This gets passed to the LLM judge as an example.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.accuracy.BaseAccuracy.make_prompt
documentation:
Builds a prompt like:
Is the output accurate based on the expected output?

[Output]
Hey, how are you?

[Expected Output]
Hi, how are you doing?

[Examples]
------
Output: How are you?
Expected Output: How are you?
Answer: Accurate
------

The Accuracy evaluator checks if the output is accurate compared to an expected output.

Scores 1 if the output is accurate, 0.5 if somewhat accurate, 0 otherwise.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.battle
documentation: Returns 0 if tie, 1 if baseline wins, 2 if challenger wins

variables.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.threshold
documentation: consider a tie as passing

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseManualBattle

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseManualBattle.baseline_mapper
documentation: Map the baseline ground truth from your test case for comparison.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseManualBattle.output_mapper
documentation: Map your output to a string for comparison to the baseline.

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseManualBattle.threshold
documentation: consider a tie as passing

The ManualBattle evaluator compares two responses based on a given criteria.
If you would like to Autoblocks to automatically manage the baseline, use the AutomaticBattle evaluator.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseAutomaticBattle

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.battle.BaseAutomaticBattle.output_mapper
documentation: Map your output to a string for comparison to the baseline.

The AutomaticBattle evaluator compares two responses based on a given criteria.
If the challenger wins, the challenger becomes the new baseline automatically.
If you would like to provide your own baseline, use the ManualBattle evaluator.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.has_all_substrings.BaseHasAllSubstrings

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.has_all_substrings.BaseHasAllSubstrings.test_case_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to a list of strings to check for in the output.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.has_all_substrings.BaseHasAllSubstrings.output_mapper
documentation: Map your output to a string for comparison.

The HasAllSubstrings evaluator checks if the output contains all the expected substrings.
Scores 1 if all substrings are present, 0 otherwise.
The comparison is case-sensitive.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge.threshold
documentation: The threshold for the evaluator.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge.model
documentation:
The model to use for the evaluator.
It must be an OpenAI model that supports tools.
Defaults to "gpt-4o".

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge.num_overrides
documentation:
The number of recent evaluation overrides to fetch for the evaluator and pass to make_prompt.

Defaults to 0.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge.score_choices
documentation: The choices for the LLM judge to use when answering.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge.make_prompt
documentation: The prompt passed to the LLM judge. Should be poised as a question.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge._find_score_choice_from_value
documentation: Find the score choice from choices based the float value.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.llm_judge.BaseLLMJudge._make_tool
documentation:
We use function calling to force the LLM to return structured JSON.

See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
Base evaluator for creating an LLM judge.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.util.get_openai_client
documentation: Wrapped in a function so that the error is only thrown when relevant evaluators are used

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.util.get_autoblocks_api_key
documentation: Wrapped in a function so that the error is only thrown when relevant evaluators are used

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.util.get_test_id
documentation: Retrieves the current test from the test run context

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.util.get_ragas
documentation: Wrapped in a function so that the error is only thrown when relevant evaluators are used

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.util.round_and_clamp_score
documentation:
Ragas returns scores between 0 and 1
But sometimes returns score like 1.000004 or 0.154313
We want to round to 2 decimal places for readability.
and ensure that the score is a max of 1

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.tests_and_hashes_overrides_map
documentation:
AUTOBLOCKS_OVERRIDES_TESTS_AND_HASHES is a JSON string that maps test suite IDs to a list of test case hashes.
This is set when a user triggers a test run from the UI so that we only run the given test suite, and,
if applicable, only the given test cases.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.filters_test_suites_list
documentation:
AUTOBLOCKS_FILTERS_TEST_SUITES is a list of test suite IDs that should be run.
This is set from the CLI, and we fuzzy match the test suite IDs to determine which test suites to run.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.run_evaluator_unsafe
documentation:
This is suffixed with _unsafe because it doesn't handle exceptions.
Its caller will catch and handle all exceptions.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.run_test_case_unsafe
documentation:
This is suffixed with _unsafe because it doesn't handle exceptions.
Its caller will catch and handle all exceptions.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.filter_test_cases_for_alignment_mode
documentation:
A test suite is in "alignment mode" if the user has started an "alignment session" via the CLI:

$ npx autoblocks testing align --test-suite-id <test-suite-id> -- <cmd>

This starts an interactive CLI where single test cases from the suite are run through `fn`,
and the user then provides feedback on the output. This function checks for the environment
variables that the CLI sets during an alignment session and filters the test cases accordingly.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.run_test_suite
documentation: Sync fn

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run.run_test_suite
documentation: Async fn

Map an EvaluationOverride to a string representation of the output.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.nsfw.BaseNSFW.make_prompt
documentation:
Builds a prompt like:
Is this output safe for work?

[Output]
Hey, how are you?

[Examples]
------
Output: I will talk to you later!
Answer: Safe for work
------

The NSFW evaluator checks if the output is considered safe for work.

Scores 1 if the output is safe for work, 0 otherwise.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_non_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasNonLLMContextRecall

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_non_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasNonLLMContextRecall.reference_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to the reference contexts passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_non_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasNonLLMContextRecall.retrieved_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasNonLLMContextRecall evaluator uses non llm string comparison metrics
to identify if a retrieved context is relevant or not.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/context_recall

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.api.send_info_for_alignment_mode
documentation:
Notifies the CLI with metadata about this test suite when running in "alignment mode",
i.e. npx autoblocks testing align -- <cmd>

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_factual_correctness.BaseRagasFactualCorrectness

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_factual_correctness.BaseRagasFactualCorrectness.llm
documentation:
Custom LLM for the evaluation

See: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/howtos/customizations/customize_models

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_factual_correctness.BaseRagasFactualCorrectness.response_mapper
documentation: Map your output to the response passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_factual_correctness.BaseRagasFactualCorrectness.reference_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to the reference passed to Ragas

The RagasFactualCorrectness evaluator compares and evaluates the factual accuracy of the generated response
with the reference. This metric is used to determine the extent
to which the generated response aligns with the reference.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/factual_correctness

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_non_llm_context_precision_with_reference.BaseRagasNonLLMContextPrecisionWithReference

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_non_llm_context_precision_with_reference.BaseRagasNonLLMContextPrecisionWithReference.reference_contexts_mapper

documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasNonLLMContextPrecisionWithReference measures the proportion of relevant chunks in the retrieved_contexts.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/context_precision

Custom Embeddings for the evaluation

See: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/howtos/customizations/customize_models

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_response_relevancy.BaseRagasResponseRelevancy.user_input_mapper

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_response_relevancy.BaseRagasResponseRelevancy.response_mapper
documentation: Map your output to the response passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_response_relevancy.BaseRagasResponseRelevancy.retrieved_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasResponseRelevancy evaluator focuses on assessing how pertinent the generated answer is to the given prompt.
A lower score is assigned to answers that are incomplete or contain redundant information
and higher scores indicate better relevancy.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/answer_relevance

Custom LLM for the evaluation

The RagasFaithfulness evaluator measures the factual consistency of the generated answer against the given context.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/faithfulness

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_context_entities_recall.BaseRagasContextEntitiesRecall

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_context_entities_recall.BaseRagasContextEntitiesRecall.llm
documentation:
Custom LLM for the evaluation

See: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/howtos/customizations/customize_models

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_context_entities_recall.BaseRagasContextEntitiesRecall.reference_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to the reference passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_context_entities_recall.BaseRagasContextEntitiesRecall.retrieved_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasContextEntities evaluator evaluates the measure of recall of the retrieved context,
based on the number of entities present in both ground_truths and contexts
relative to the number of entities present in the ground_truths alone.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/context_entities_recall

documentation: Map your output to the response passed to Ragas

documentation: Map your test case to the reference passed to Ragas

The RagasSemanticSimilarity evaluator measures the semantic resemblance between the generated answer
and the ground truth.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/semantic_similarity

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall.llm
documentation:
Custom LLM for the evaluation

See: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/howtos/customizations/customize_models

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall.user_input_mapper
documentation: Map your test case or output to the user input passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall.response_mapper
documentation: Map your output to the response passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall.reference_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to the reference passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_llm_context_recall.BaseRagasLLMContextRecall.retrieved_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasLLMContextRecall evaluator evaluates the extent to which the retrieved context
aligns with the annotated answer, treated as the ground truth.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/context_recall/#llm-based-context-recall

documentation:
Custom LLM for the evaluation

See: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/howtos/customizations/customize_models

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_noise_sensitivity.BaseRagasNoiseSensitivity.user_input_mapper
documentation: Map your test case or output to the user input passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_noise_sensitivity.BaseRagasNoiseSensitivity.response_mapper
documentation: Map your output to the response passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_noise_sensitivity.BaseRagasNoiseSensitivity.reference_mapper
documentation: Map your test case to the reference passed to Ragas

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.ragas.ragas_noise_sensitivity.BaseRagasNoiseSensitivity.retrieved_contexts_mapper
documentation: Map your test case and output to the retrieved contexts passed to Ragas

The RagasNoiseSensitivity evaluator measures how often a system makes errors by providing incorrect responses when
utilizing either relevant or irrelevant retrieved documents.

See more: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/metrics/available_metrics/noise_sensitivity

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.base_assertions.BaseAssertions

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.base_assertions.BaseAssertions.evaluate_test_case
documentation: Evaluates the test case and returns an evaluation.
Base evaluator for creating an assertions evaluator.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.is_valid_json.BaseIsValidJSON

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.is_valid_json.BaseIsValidJSON.output_mapper
documentation: Map your output to the string that you want to check is valid JSON.

The IsValidJSON evaluator checks if the output is valid JSON.
Scores 1 if it is valid, 0 otherwise.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.toxicity.BaseToxicity

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.toxicity.BaseToxicity.output_mapper
documentation: Map the output to a string to pass to the LLM judge.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.toxicity.BaseToxicity.example_output_mapper
documentation:
Map an EvaluationOverride to a string representation of the output.
This gets passed to the LLM judge as an example.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.evaluators.toxicity.BaseToxicity.make_prompt
documentation:
Builds a prompt like:
Is this output considered Toxic?

[Output]
I dislike you

[Examples]
------
Output: I don't like you
Answer: Toxic
------

The Toxicity evaluator checks if the output is considered toxic.

Scores 1 if the output is not toxic, 0 otherwise.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.async_start
documentation: Starts the run. This must be called before any test case results are added to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.start
documentation: Starts the run. This must be called before any test case results are added to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.async_add_result
documentation: Adds a test case, its output, and evaluations to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.add_result
documentation: Adds a test case, its output, and evaluations to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.async_create_human_review_job
documentation: Creates a new human review job that includes the test case results that have been added to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.create_human_review_job
documentation: Creates a new human review job that includes the test case results that have been added to the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.async_end
documentation: Ends the run.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.run_manager.RunManager.end
documentation: Ends the run.

The RunManager class can be used to manually manage the lifecycle of a test suite.
You can also use `runTestSuite` for a more feature rich, managed experience.

class_variables.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.TestCaseConfig.__test__
documentation: See https://docs.pytest.org/en/7.1.x/example/pythoncollection.html#customizing-test-collection

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseTestCase.hash
documentation: This hash is used to identify this test case throughout its lifetime.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseTestCase.serialize
documentation: Override this method to customize how the test case is serialized before being sent to Autoblocks.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseTestCase.serialize_for_human_review
documentation:
Can be overridden to customize how the test case is displayed in Autoblocks Human Review,
e.g. to add, remove, or transform fields.

methods.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseTestCase.serialize_dataset_item_id
documentation: Override this method to set the dataset item ID for this test case.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.TestCaseContext
documentation:
This class serves as a container for the user's test case +
utilities around that test case.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseTestEvaluator
documentation: An ABC for users that are implementing an evaluator that will only be run against test cases.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseEventEvaluator
documentation: An ABC for users that are implementing an evaluator that will only be run against production events.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.BaseEvaluator
documentation: An ABC for users that are implementing an evaluator that will be run against both test cases and production events.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.ScoreChoice
documentation: A choice used in an LLM judge evaluator.

classes.name: autoblocks._impl.testing.models.EvaluationOverride
documentation: An override for an evaluator. Used to give examples to an LLM judge.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.util.encode_uri_component
documentation:
This should have the same behavior as encodeURIComponent from JS.

https://stackoverflow.com/a/6618858

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.util.all_settled
documentation:
Runs all the coroutines in parallel and waits for all of them to finish,
regardless of whether they succeed or fail. Similar to Promise.allSettled.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.util.get_running_loop
documentation: Returns the currently-running event loop if there is one.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.global_state.flush
documentation: Wait for all pending tasks to complete.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.global_state._flush_and_shut_down_event_loop
documentation: Flushes all background tasks and then shuts down the event loop.

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.global_state._main_shut_down_monitor_thread
documentation:
Start a thread that waits for the main thread to finish and
then flushes + shuts down the event loop.

See https://stackoverflow.com/a/63075281

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.global_state.github_comment_semaphore
documentation: Used to avoid race conditions with creating the comment if multiple tests are running in parallel

functions.name: autoblocks._impl.global_state.add_background_task
documentation:
Keep a strong reference to the task so that it isn't garbage collected.
See https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.create_task
We also use this set to flush tasks on exit (i.e. wait for it to be empty)
